---
title: "plot"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(here)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)

# 2. Define the path relative to the project root
file_path <- here("data", "test_data.csv")

# 3. Read the data
study_data <- read_csv(file_path)

# 2. Convert to factors and set logical orders
# This ensures that "No Prep" is the reference level for your models
# and that "Tired -> Neutral -> Hyper" follows a logical progression.
study_data <- study_data %>%
  mutate(
    baseline_state = factor(baseline_state, levels = c("tired", "neutral", "hyper")),
    prep = factor(prep, levels = c("No Prep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "Child Choice")),
    child_id = as.factor(child_id)
  )

# 3. Quick verification
print(summary(study_data))
head(study_data)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(study_data, aes(x = prep, y = engagement, fill = prep)) +
  # Use boxplots to show distribution
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  # Add jittered points to show every single session's data
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.1, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  # Facet by Child (Rows) and Baseline State (Columns)
  facet_grid(child_id ~ baseline_state) +
  # Styling
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = 1:5, limits = c(0.5, 5.5)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Personalized Prep Impact on Session Engagement",
    subtitle = "Faceted by Child and Arrival Baseline State",
    x = "Preparation Type",
    y = "BRES-5 Engagement Score",
    fill = "Condition"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```
